economic_notes: Malahat Nation has extensive business interests including construction, forestry, fuel, seafood, film studios, chemical, battery technologies, business park development, and real estate LPs. GBEs not fully audited due to unavailable financial information. Note receivable to GBEs of $39.9M bearing 3.1% interest. FNFA promissory notes totaling $43.5M. Construction projects including Business Park, Boat Launch, 108 Maple Leaf, 2SLGBTQIA+ Building underway.
notes: Audit is qualified because financial information for several GBEs and partnerships is unavailable and has not been fully accounted for in the consolidated statements - consistent with prior year qualification. Nation has 35 related entities listed, only 1 fully consolidated (Malahat Nation Community Development Society), remainder accounted for by modified equity method or excluded. Promissory notes payable to FNFA total $43.45M including a $6.44M demand note at 6.25%. Note receivable from GBEs totals $39.9M. OSR calculated as total revenue ($16,097,526) minus identified government transfers (ISC $3,597,624 + ISC recovery -$58,591 + CMHC $299,785 + DFO $2,250,978 + Transport Canada $461,872 + Gov Canada Other $723,094 + Parks Canada $82,956 + FNHA $2,278,214 + Province BC $1,682,422 + NRCan $49,999 + ESDC $25,000 = $11,393,353 net of deferred revenue adjustments makes direct calculation complex; OSR estimated from non-government lines). Deferred revenue movement is complex with large gross flows. Capital lease obligations of $101,531 also outstanding.
